steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The goal is to specify how long Despina's orbit of Neptune takes. The relevant note is: "Despina completes an orbit of Neptune in 0.33 Earth days on average." The Kepler's laws notes provide background but don't state a duration.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — "Among Neptune's fourteen moons is Despina, which completes its orbit of the planet in 0.33 Earth days on average": directly states the orbital duration using the exact figure from the notes. ✓
  • Choice B — "Despina's orbit can be described using Kepler's three laws": mentions Kepler's laws but never states how long the orbit takes. ✗
  • Choice C — "It takes Despina three Earth days to orbit Neptune": states an incorrect duration (three days instead of 0.33 days) and contradicts the notes. ✗
  • Choice D — "Kepler's three laws describe orbits, including Despina's, which takes 0.33 Earth days": buries the duration inside an irrelevant frame about Kepler's laws, making Kepler the focus rather than Despina's orbit. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
